choice: Yes — add ONE generic 'One-Time Event' entity in v1: {date/age, signed amount expressed in real or nominal dollars, direction in/out, target Account or tax bucket for an inflow / source for an outflow, a taxable-amount field that feeds the existing bracketed-net tax rule (dc489f83), optional link to a real-estate Holding it liquidates}. Inflows land in the chosen account and then grow/are-sourced by the normal engine; outflows hit the withdrawal order like a spending spike. Do NOT model the triggering event's own mechanics (no capital-gains-on-home-sale calc beyond the net taxable amount you type, no step-up, no RSU vesting schedule) — keep it a dated signed cashflow, consistent with the bracketed-net fidelity cap.
rationale: This is a first-order driver competitors (ProjectionLab, BoldIn, Root) all expose, and the two recurring primitives can't represent it cleanly: a one-time home-sale inflow can't be a recurring stream, and a 4-year college spike distorts the 'baseline real spending' semantics if you bake it into the spending path. It also unlocks the only sanctioned way to convert a non-depleting real-estate line (af999d56) or net out a mortgage payoff (d87af9b2) inside the projection. A single signed-cashflow entity adds one engine path, not several, and rides entirely on machinery already decided (withdrawal order fe56f1d6, bracketed-net tax dc489f83, account buckets d424a37f). The alternative — encoding spikes inside the spending schedule and ignoring lumpy inflows — silently overstates depletion risk for any household with a house to sell or an inheritance coming.
